problem.title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
problem.description: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list k at a time, and return the modified list.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Iterative
solutions.description: Count k nodes ahead before committing to a reversal; if fewer than k nodes remain, leave the tail as-is. After reversing each group, reconnect the preceding segment's tail to the new group head and advance past the now-reversed group, repeating until the list is exhausted.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def reverseKGroup(head: Optional[object], k: int) -> Optional[object]:
    dummy = type('ListNode', (), {'val': 0, 'next': head})()
    prev = dummy

    current = head
    while current:
        # Check if k nodes remain
        tail = current
        count = 0
        while tail and count < k:
            tail = tail.next
            count += 1

        if count < k:
            break  # Fewer than k nodes left; leave as-is

        # Reverse the k-node group [current, tail)
        prev_node, node = None, current
        while node is not tail:
            nxt = node.next
            node.next = prev_node
            prev_node = node
            node = nxt

        # Reconnect
        prev.next = prev_node   # new group head
        current.next = tail     # old group head is now the tail
        prev = current
        current = tail

    return dummy.next
